Live public-data lesson

Hilsa, Nalanda, Bihar

This story joins live MWS geometry, tehsil-wide analytical tables, watershed time series, generated layer URLs, and a report handoff into one continuous workflow. The composite score shown here is exploratory and intentionally transparent: it combines runoff, surface water, cropping intensity, protection opportunity, and SOGE with light pressure penalties from urbanization and deforestation.

Generated on 2026-03-16 04:12 UTC from https://api-doc.core-stack.org/.

Why this MWS rises

Runoff signal: 69.4 mm
Surface water signal: 19.01 ha
Cropping intensity: 1.69
Protection area: 1330.92 ha
SOGE development: 85.99%
Urbanization pressure: 8.67 ha
Deforestation pressure: 8.85 ha

Report handoff

https://api-doc.core-stack.org/api/v1/generate_mws_report/?state=bihar&district=nalanda&block=hilsa&uid=12_321370

Reusable download surfaces

The same location exposes raster downloads for QGIS and local analysis, vector WFS layers for direct GeoJSON use, and GEE asset paths for cloud-side reuse. These are the exact surfaces the companion lesson page teaches people to move between.

Dataset Type Layer name Layer URL GEE asset path
MWS vector mws_nalanda_hilsa download projects/ee-corestackdev/assets/apps/mws/bihar/nalanda/hilsa/filtered_mws_nalanda_hilsa_uid
Surface Water Bodies vector surface_waterbodies_nalanda_hilsa download projects/ee-corestackdev/assets/apps/mws/bihar/nalanda/hilsa/swb2_nalanda_hilsa
SOGE vector soge_vector_nalanda_hilsa download projects/ee-corestackdev/assets/apps/mws/bihar/nalanda/hilsa/soge_vector_nalanda_hilsa
Restoration Vector vector restoration_nalanda_hilsa_vector download projects/ee-corestackdev/assets/apps/mws/bihar/nalanda/hilsa/restoration_nalanda_hilsa_vector
Restoration Raster raster restoration_nalanda_hilsa_raster download projects/ee-corestackdev/assets/apps/mws/bihar/nalanda/hilsa/restoration_nalanda_hilsa_raster
Stream Order vector stream_order_nalanda_hilsa_raster download projects/ee-corestackdev/assets/apps/mws/bihar/nalanda/hilsa/stream_order_nalanda_hilsa_raster

What to do next

  • Open the joined GeoJSON in QGIS to style MWS boundaries by planning signal.
  • Use the generated layer URLs to pull a raster or vector layer into a notebook or GIS.
  • Use the GEE asset paths to continue the same analysis in Earth Engine.
  • Open the MWS report URL to shift from exploration into reporting and planning.
  • Swap the location arguments and rerun the script for a different state, district, and tehsil.
